verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes pro-epiregulin, a 162-aa type I single-pass transmembrane proprotein. The mature EGF-like domain is displayed in the extracellular space, anchored by the protein's own TM domain. This proform is stably present at the outer face of the plasma membrane on expressing cells, where it serves as substrate for ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding. The proform is not transiently recruited by another surface protein — it traffics to the PM via its own TM anchor. Both the proform (surface-displayed) and shed mature form (soluble) are pharmacologically relevant. This meets the 'yes' bar as a type I transmembrane protein with a stably surface-accessible ectodomain.
